candidate_families.reason: Reclaim-quality features point to a policy filter, but it must not hide weak breadth.
Break-quality gates may reduce the tail, but exact-bucket attribution makes overfit risk high.
Validation slicing may explain path fragility, but reserved-data use is blocked until CPCV improves.
Worst-path losses are concentrated by setup subtype, so test structural subtype hypotheses before narrower gates.
Label redesign is justified only when high-confidence losses pair with failed calibration.
Threshold-only work is weak because losses occur in the high-confidence bucket unless calibration itself fails.
CPCV failure blocks model escalation and reserved-data progression.
Broader structural features are the fallback when attribution is incomplete or too narrow.
CPCV failure blocks model escalation and reserved-data progression.
Setup search is expensive and should wait unless failures point to the setup definition itself.
